summary.intent: Clean up legacy Session table/hook handler code from Gossamer, fix git user attribution per-checkpoint, fix polling loop bug, and add search-result scroll-to-highlight navigation in the session detail UI.
summary.outcome: All goals achieved: hook handler and SDK CLI deleted, schema stripped to only indexer-relevant tables, git user sourced from commit author in CheckpointMetadata, polling loop fixed to reset to remote branch, and full scroll+highlight+auto-expand flow implemented for all content types (text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result).

repo: FTS5 virtual tables (LogContentFts and its shadow tables) must be dropped manually via sqlite3 before every `prisma db push` — Prisma cannot handle them and will error out.

The old hook handler (handler.ts) stored git user, cwd, summary etc. in the Session table as a cross-invocation scratchpad; all of this was redundant since the Event table already held the source data.
CheckpointMetadata is the correct place to store per-checkpoint git author info; it's populated at index time via findCommitForCheckpoint in indexer.ts.
The server reads gitUser from local git config at startup; this is wrong for multi-user repos — per-checkpoint author must come from CheckpointMetadata.gitUserName/gitUserEmail.
The poll loop was broken: it fetched origin but then reset to the local branch tip (entire/checkpoints/v1) instead of origin/entire/checkpoints/v1, so remote commits were silently discarded.
logEventsToEvents produces synthetic events with negative IDs; the real LogEvent.id must be threaded as _sourceLogEventId to enable DOM scroll targeting.
Mantine ScrollArea renders its own scrollable viewport div — window.scrollIntoView() has no effect; must use viewportRef and manual offset calculation.
Thinking blocks in Claude responses are indexed by FTS but were never rendered in the UI; they needed to be surfaced as a collapsible section in ClaudeTurnCard.
ToolGroupItem has two nesting levels: top-level group toggle and per-tool-call individual toggles; auto-expand must target only the specific matching tool call, not all of them.
Stop events in logEventsToEvents should carry _sourceLogEventId pointing to the assistant LogEvent (not the system stop), since that's where the matched text lives.

code.finding: logEventsToEvents must accumulate all assistant log event IDs per turn (currentTurnLogEventIds) including thinking-only events, and attach them to the Stop event as _extraSourceLogEventIds so thinking search hits resolve to the right card.
HighlightCode children must be typed as string, not ReactNode — Glob/Grep cases previously passed JSX arrays causing text.split to throw at runtime.
Receives targetLogEventId to check each tool's pre/post event IDs individually — only the matching ToolUseItem auto-expands, not all tools in the group.

Scroll-to-mark flow is two-phase: (1) set highlightActive at 150ms to render marks and trigger Collapse animations, (2) useEffect fires after highlightActive commit, waits 250ms for animations to settle, then uses requestAnimationFrame to measure final mark position before scrolling.
Highlight terms injected via React context so all text leaf nodes in the markdown renderer can apply split/mark highlighting without prop-drilling through every node type.
Poll loop fix: must reset to origin/${CHECKPOINT_BRANCH} after successful fetch, with fallback to local branch only if remote is unreachable.

workflow: When Prisma can't handle schema changes (FTS5 tables), drop them manually with sqlite3 first, then run db push.
After killing a background server with pkill, always verify with lsof -i :PORT that nothing is still listening — stale processes can survive pkill pattern mismatches.
Running npm commands from the wrong directory (after cd for type-check) silently fails with 'Missing script' rather than an obvious path error.
For scroll-to-element in animated Collapse sections, a two-phase approach (render marks first, measure positions after animation settles) is more reliable than a single delayed scrollIntoView.

friction: FTS5 virtual tables blocked prisma db push twice — no way to automate this without a pre-push script.
Old handler.js process survived pkill because the pattern didn't match; required manual lsof + kill.
Server was accidentally started from app/ directory after a type-check cd, causing a confusing 'Missing script' failure.
scrollIntoView on the card div centered the top of a tall card, not the actual match — required switching to mark-element targeting with animation delay.
ToolUseItem HighlightCode received ReactNode children (JSX array) instead of a string, causing a runtime text.split crash that required type tightening to prevent.
targetLogEventId was referenced inside ClaudeTurnCard without being passed as a prop, causing a ReferenceError at runtime.
open_items: Existing stale rows in old tables (Session, Event, AgentParent, PendingToolUse, Checkpoint family) are still in the live DB — prisma db push with --accept-data-loss will drop them on next deployment.
git user info in CheckpointMetadata will only be populated for checkpoints indexed after this change — historical sessions will fall back to local git config until re-indexed.

User message text (EventItem/UserPromptCard) gets highlight treatment but the MarkdownView highlight context is not applied there — only plain text split/mark is used, so markdown in user messages won't highlight across node boundaries.
Shadow sessions (not yet committed to a checkpoint) have no git author and will always fall back to local git config for user display.
